page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 22 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The processor is equipped with 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The system is configured with 176 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU.

This instance includes 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 8 local SSDs, making it highly suitable for applications requiring high-speed local disk access. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, such as image processing with a Geekbench score of 16162.0 and CPU encryption with a Passmark score of 38720.91. Memory bandwidth is measured at 2998.96 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 111.44 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

While the server performs well in general-purpose tasks and small-to-medium language model inference, achieving 175.16 tokens/sec for 7B model prompt processing, it exhibits limitations in larger workloads. Specifically, text generation for a 70B LLM model drops to a poor rate of 2.37 tokens/sec. Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and substantial local NVMe storage, this server is optimized for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications rather than large-scale AI model execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 44 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 176 GB of RAM. It includes 3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives, offering significant local capacity for data-intensive applications. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in image processing and CPU encryption, alongside solid memory bandwidth of 111.44 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, testing reveals performance tradeoffs, particularly in large-scale AI workloads where 70B LLM text generation drops to 2.37 tokens/sec. This instance is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, and general enterprise workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd features 44 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for database and general workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44-lssd is a 44-vCPU virtual machine with 176 GB RAM and 3216 GB local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and over three terabytes of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz
176 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 integrated drives
Strong performance in image processing and cryptographic encryption benchmarks
Tradeoffs in large-scale AI workloads with slow 70B LLM text generation
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose computing
